target.name: Hyperscale Power
target.founder: Daniel Rothmund (PhD ETH Zürich, ex-ABB), Sami Pettersson (ex-ABB)

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Hyperscale Power is a seed-stage climate and energy company founded by former ABB executives with €5M in total raised capital. The target operates within a fragmented frontier-tech sector comprising nine same-sector peers, including one public comparable (LNZA) and eight private peers. Leading private comparables include Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen, though these operate across heterogeneous subsectors within climate and energy.

Peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable to this analysis. A structured search for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier peers within Climate & Energy yielded fewer than three datapoints in each category, falling below the minimum threshold required for meaningful peer triangulation. An all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the wide dispersion in business models, technology maturity, and capital requirements across the broader peer universe.

Without sufficient comparable transaction or valuation data at the seed stage in this subsector, traditional triangulation methods cannot be reliably applied. A credible valuation narrative for Hyperscale Power would require alternative approaches—including DCF analysis tied to explicit technical and commercialization milestones, or a detailed breakdown of founder experience and IP defensibility—rather than reliance on statistical peer analysis. The absence of narrow peer data underscores both the frontier nature of the sector and the substantial uncertainty surrounding early-stage valuation in climate tech.
